title: cineraria parvifolia
intro: Cineraria parvifolia, also known as Babchi, is a small, annual plant native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The medicinal properties of Cineraria parvifolia lie in its ability to stimulate skin cell regeneration and improve wound healing, due to the presence of scopoletin, a coumarin derivative. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat skin conditions such as psoriasis, eczema, and leucoderma, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Cineraria parvifolia can be prepared into herbal remedies such as oils, powders, and creams, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
